meta_title: Impact Doors in Westlake, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Westlake, FL. ESW & CWI brands, 140mph rated, in-house install. Free quotes, financing from $87/mo.
h1: Impact Doors for Westlake Homes — Built to Outlast Every Storm Season
intro:
Westlake, Florida is one of Palm Beach County's most exciting master-planned communities, attracting upper-middle-class families to neighborhoods like Arden and Cresswind with resort-style amenities, top-rated schools, and beautifully crafted homes averaging $500,000 or more in value. It's a community built with intention — and your home's protection should be no different. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, known throughout South Florida as 'The Kings of Service,' brings that same level of intention to every impact door installation we complete in Westlake.

Located in ZIP code 33470, Westlake sits approximately 20 miles inland from the Atlantic coast, but don't let that distance create a false sense of security. Palm Beach County's interior communities are fully exposed to the sustained winds and intense pressure fluctuations that accompany major hurricane landfalls. Westlake is designated within a 140 mph wind zone under the Florida Building Code, meaning every exterior door on your property must meet rigorous impact-resistance standards. Standard entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors simply do not meet these requirements — and in a real storm, they become the most dangerous point of failure in your home's envelope.

Whether you live along the tree-lined streets of Arden, enjoy the active lifestyle amenities of Cresswind, or are building new near Westlake Town Center, upgrading to certified impact doors is one of the smartest investments you can make for your family's safety and your home's long-term value. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing specializes in the full range of impact-resistant entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors, all installed by our own fully trained, in-house crew with more than 60 years of combined experience across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties. We don't subcontract, we don't cut corners, and we stand behind every installation with the kind of service reputation that has earned us an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau.

section_why.h2: Why Westlake Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Hurricane Door Installation
section_why.body:
Choosing the right contractor for impact door installation in Westlake isn't just about finding the lowest price — it's about finding a company that understands the local building environment, the permit process through the City of Westlake Building Department, and the specific performance standards required by Florida Building Code in Palm Beach County. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing checks every one of those boxes, and then some.

Our installation team is 100% in-house. That means when Premier Impact Windows & Roofing sends a crew to your home in Arden or Cresswind, those are our employees — people who have been trained, vetted, and held to our internal quality standards on every job. We've completed 500+ home improvement projects across South Florida and have developed deep familiarity with Palm Beach County's permit and inspection process. We handle all permitting paperwork on your behalf, coordinate with the City of Westlake Building Department, and ensure your installation passes inspection the first time.

We carry ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI product lines — two of the most respected names in Florida's impact door industry — so Westlake homeowners receive products that are rigorously tested, code-compliant, and engineered specifically for Florida's climate. From multi-point locking systems on entry doors to heavy-duty track systems on sliding glass doors, every product we install delivers both security and performance.

Finally, we understand what a $500,000 home means to the families who own them. These aren't just investment properties — they're where you raise your children, host your holidays, and build your life. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing treats every home in Westlake with that level of respect. From the initial consultation to the final walk-through, our goal is to earn the kind of referral that only comes from a truly exceptional customer experience. That's what 'The Kings of Service' means to us.

section_history.h2: Storm History in Westlake: What Hurricane Irma Taught Palm Beach County Homeowners
section_history.body:
Westlake is a relatively young city — much of its residential development has occurred in the past decade — but the land it sits on has a clear storm history that every homeowner should understand. Palm Beach County has experienced four named storms since 1990, with Hurricane Irma in 2017 being the most instructive recent example of how inland communities can suffer serious wind and structural damage even without a direct coastal landfall.

When Irma made its way up Florida's peninsula in September 2017, communities throughout Palm Beach County experienced sustained winds that overwhelmed standard doors and windows across tens of thousands of homes. The storm's size and slow movement meant extended wind exposure — a punishing condition that tests every weak point in a home's envelope. Standard entry doors blew in. Sliding glass doors failed under pressure differentials. French doors separated from their frames. The resulting interior damage — to flooring, drywall, furniture, and personal property — often exceeded the cost of the doors themselves many times over.

For Westlake homeowners, many of whom moved into newly built homes after 2017, the lesson from Irma is that building code minimums matter, but proactive protection is even better. With only about 5% of local homes predating the landmark 1994 Florida Building Code revisions, most Westlake residences were built to modern standards — but not all builders or original owners invested in the highest level of opening protection. Upgrading to certified impact doors from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing ensures your home is prepared for the next Irma-level event, whether it arrives from the Atlantic or the Gulf.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors in Westlake and Palm Beach County
section_code.body:
Westlake falls outside Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), which applies specifically to Miami-Dade and Broward counties. However, that distinction does not reduce the performance requirements placed on impact doors in Palm Beach County — it simply means the applicable standards are governed by the Florida Building Code (FBC) rather than the more stringent Miami-Dade NOA (Notice of Acceptance) system. Impact doors installed in Westlake must still be tested and certified to withstand a 140 mph design wind speed, and they must carry valid Florida Product Approvals demonstrating compliance with FBC Chapter 16 structural load requirements.

For homeowners in Westlake's 33470 ZIP code, all exterior door replacements require a permit through the City of Westlake Building Department. This is not optional — unpermitted work can result in stop-work orders, fines, and serious complications when you attempt to sell your home or file an insurance claim. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permit application process on your behalf, from preparing the necessary product documentation to scheduling required inspections. Our teams are fully licensed and insured in Palm Beach County and maintain an up-to-date working knowledge of the City of Westlake's specific review procedures.

Beyond minimum code compliance, Florida law also requires that any opening protection product used in a permitted installation carry a current Florida Product Approval number. All ESW and CWI products we install carry these approvals and are tested under ASTM E1886 and ASTM E1996 protocols — the industry-standard large-missile impact tests that simulate real-world debris conditions during a hurricane. When your doors carry these certifications, you can have confidence that they've been tested to perform when it matters most.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Performance for Westlake's Finest Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two of the most trusted impact door lines available in Florida: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands are engineered specifically for the demands of the Florida market and offer a range of styles that complement the architectural aesthetics found throughout Westlake's neighborhoods — from the craftsman-influenced streetscapes of Arden to the elegant elevation profiles common in Cresswind.

ESW's impact door collection includes entry doors, French doors, and sliding glass doors constructed with heavy-gauge aluminum frames and laminated safety glass that maintains its integrity even when struck by large windborne debris. Their multi-point locking systems engage at multiple points along the door frame — not just at the handle — distributing load across the entire opening and dramatically reducing the risk of door separation under hurricane wind pressure. ESW products carry Florida Product Approvals for wind speeds well in excess of Westlake's 140 mph design requirement, giving homeowners a meaningful margin of safety above the code minimum.

CWI products offer a complementary lineup with particular strength in the sliding glass door and large-opening categories — a critical consideration for Westlake homes that often feature wide rear lanai openings, open-concept great rooms, and panoramic backyard views. CWI's sliding impact doors use reinforced interlock systems, stainless steel hardware, and dual-pane laminated glass to provide not only hurricane protection but also year-round energy efficiency and noise reduction. Both ESW and CWI products are available in a range of finishes, glass options, and frame colors to ensure your new impact doors enhance — not compromise — your home's curb appeal and interior design.

section_cost.h2: What Do Impact Doors Cost in Westlake — and How Do You Finance Them?
section_cost.body:
For homeowners in Westlake with properties averaging $500,000 in value, impact door installation represents a small but strategically important fraction of that investment. The actual cost depends on the number and size of openings, the product line selected, and the complexity of the installation. A single impact entry door installation in Westlake typically ranges from $1,500 to $4,000 fully installed. Sliding glass doors and French doors, due to their larger size and more complex hardware requirements, can range from $3,000 to $8,000 or more per opening. Full-home door packages — replacing every exterior opening with certified impact products — commonly range from $8,000 to $20,000 depending on the home's configuration.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes this investment accessible through flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down available for qualified homeowners. Our financing partners offer competitive rates and terms that allow Westlake families to move forward with impact door upgrades without waiting years to accumulate the full project cost. Given that most homeowners recoup significant value through insurance savings, energy efficiency gains, and increased resale price, the net cost of delaying this upgrade often exceeds the cost of acting now.

From a return-on-investment perspective, impact doors in Palm Beach County are among the highest-ROI home improvements available. Studies consistently show that impact-rated opening protection adds measurable value to Florida homes at resale — particularly in communities like Westlake where buyer expectations and home values are elevated. Combined with insurance premium reductions that can reach hundreds of dollars annually, the financial case for impact door installation is compelling for virtually every homeowner in the 33470 area.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings for Westlake Homeowners Who Install Impact Doors
section_insurance.body:
One of the most immediate and tangible financial benefits of installing certified impact doors in Westlake is the reduction in homeowners insurance premiums. Florida's wind mitigation credit system, governed by the Office of Insurance Regulation, allows homeowners who have completed a wind mitigation inspection to submit documented proof of opening protection upgrades directly to their insurance carrier. Impact-rated doors — particularly when combined with impact windows — can qualify your home for the 'Opening Protection' credit category, which represents one of the largest single discount categories available under Florida's wind mitigation framework.

Palm Beach County homeowners are served by a range of insurance carriers, including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and several private market alternatives. Most of these carriers recognize certified impact door installations as qualifying improvements and will reduce your wind premium accordingly. In some cases, particularly for homes that previously carried hurricane shutter surcharges or lacked any opening protection credits, the annual premium reduction can range from $400 to $1,200 or more — depending on your home's replacement cost value, your current carrier's rate structure, and the extent of the opening protection installed.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides all customers with the product documentation, Florida Product Approval numbers, and installation records needed to support a wind mitigation inspection following project completion. We recommend that all Westlake homeowners schedule a wind mitigation inspection with a licensed inspector after their impact door installation is complete and permitted — this single step is what transforms your installation into documented insurance savings. For homes in Arden and Cresswind where annual insurance premiums are often substantial, the cumulative savings over a five- to ten-year period can significantly offset the original installation cost.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact doors if I live 20 miles inland in Westlake?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Distance from the coast does not eliminate hurricane wind risk in Palm Beach County. Westlake sits in a 140 mph wind zone under the Florida Building Code, and inland communities like those in the 33470 ZIP code experienced significant wind damage during Hurricane Irma in 2017. Standard doors are not rated for these wind speeds and can fail catastrophically during a major storm, allowing wind, rain, and debris to enter and destroy your home's interior.

faqs.q: How much does a typical impact door installation cost in Westlake, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Costs vary based on door type and quantity. A single impact entry door typically runs $1,500–$4,000 installed, while sliding glass and French doors can range from $3,000–$8,000 per opening. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options for qualified homeowners. Our team will provide a detailed, itemized quote so you know exactly what you're investing before any work begins.

faqs.q: Do impact door installations in Westlake require a permit from the City of Westlake Building Department?
faqs.a: Yes. All exterior door replacements in Westlake require a building permit issued by the City of Westlake Building Department, and all installed products must carry valid Florida Product Approvals. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process on your behalf — from application submission to inspection scheduling. Skipping the permit process creates liability at resale and can complicate insurance claims, so working with a licensed contractor who manages permitting is essential.

faqs.q: How long does impact door installation take in Westlake?
faqs.a: For most Westlake homes, a standard impact door installation — one to three openings — can be completed in a single day once the permit is approved and the product is in stock. Larger projects involving multiple sliding glass doors or full-home door packages may take two to three days. Permitting timelines through the City of Westlake Building Department typically add two to four weeks before installation begins. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing will provide a clear project timeline during your consultation.

faqs.q: Will installing impact doors lower my homeowners insurance premium in Westlake?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes. Florida's wind mitigation credit system rewards homeowners who install certified impact-rated opening protection with measurable insurance premium discounts. After your Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installation is permitted and complete, a licensed wind mitigation inspector can document your upgrades for submission to your carrier. Palm Beach County homeowners with full opening protection often see annual premium reductions ranging from $400 to $1,200 depending on their insurer and home's replacement value.
